concerns.text: T037 (master analysis script) is listed as incomplete, yet it is the consumer of T033 (regression), T034 (ANOVA), T035 (sensitivity), and T036 (plotting). T037 cannot be implemented or tested until T035 is complete. The current order implies T037 is a prerequisite for the 'Checkpoint' but is missing, breaking the independent testability of US3.

T044 (power analysis) is listed as incomplete. It consumes output from T033/T034. While T033/T034 are marked complete, T044 is required to validate SC-003. The 'Checkpoint' claims 'All user stories should now be independently functional,' but US3 cannot be fully validated (SC-003) without T044.

T018a (batch aggregation) is incomplete. T018 (batch generation) produces per-class batches. T028 (simulation runner) and T037 (analysis) require a 'global batch' (FR-001). T018a is the producer of the global batch artifact. Without T018a, downstream consumers (T028, T037) lack their required input artifact, violating the producer-before-consumer rule.

T028 (simulation runner) depends on T029 (result serialization) and T029a (schema validation). T029a is marked complete, but T029 is marked complete. However, T028 description says 'utilizing... T029... after core logic... serialization... and schema validation... are implemented'. The ordering is correct here, but T028 is the consumer of T018a's output (global batch). Since T018a is incomplete, T028 cannot be fully executed/tested, breaking the US2 independent testability claim.

T045 (batch validation) checks SC-001 and SC-005. SC-005 requires 'sensitivity_sweep.json' (from T035). T035 is incomplete. T045 cannot verify SC-005 until T035 is complete. The task list implies T045 is a final polish step, but it is actually a dependency gate for the validation of US3's sensitivity requirement.
